mission.title: opentelemetry-operator: TargetAllocator for daemonset
mission.description: TargetAllocator for daemonset. Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentelemetry-operator deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentelemetry-operator version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
helm list -n opentelemetry-operator 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentelemetry-operator installation.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-operator configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-operator configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-operator
```
Ideally, when deployed as a daemonset, collectors would scrape the pods running on the node
local to the daemonset instance. It's not obvious from the docs or configs as to if that is achievable
with the current targetAllocator strategies.

steps.title: Apply the fix for TargetAllocator for daemonset
steps.description:
Introduces new allocation strategy "per node" that is intended to distribute targets to collectors on the basis of the node on which the targets reside, i.e. each collector will be instructed to scrape targers running on the collector's node.

This strategy is intended to be only used with a collector in `deamonset` mode (running as agent on each node). It's not suitable for other modes.

- Added unit tests
- Also did manual testing

Updated API documentation.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-operator --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "TargetAllocator for daemonset" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Introduces new allocation strategy "per node" that is intended to distribute targets to collectors on the basis of the node on which the targets reside, i.e. each collector will be instructed to scrape targers running on the collector's node.

This strategy is intended to be only used with a collector in `deamonset` mode (running as agent on each node). It's not suitable for other modes.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-operator installed or the issue environment reproducible.
